# Feature Breakdown — Notification Preferences

<!-- Reference solution for Lab 07 Task 2. Produce your own breakdown before reading. -->
<!-- Generated by: Planner session -->
<!-- Input: labs/lab-07-multi-agent-workflow/starter/feature-request.md -->

---

## Scope of This Breakdown

Feature: Notification Preferences (opt-out per notification type, per user)
Branch: `labs/lab-07-multi-agent-workflow/starter/`
Files in scope: `notifications.py` (implementation), `tests/test_notifications.py` (tests)
Files excluded: `user.py` — read-only reference; do not modify

---

## Sub-tasks

### Sub-task 1 — Add `get_notification_preferences()`

**File:** `labs/lab-07-multi-agent-workflow/starter/notifications.py`

**Acceptance criterion:** The function `get_notification_preferences(user_id: str) -> dict[NotificationType, bool]` exists in `notifications.py`. When called with a user ID that has no entry in `_preferences`, it returns a dict with all `NotificationType` members mapped to `True`. When called with a user ID that has an entry, it returns that entry.

---

### Sub-task 2 — Add `update_notification_preferences()`

**File:** `labs/lab-07-multi-agent-workflow/starter/notifications.py`

**Acceptance criterion:** The function `update_notification_preferences(user_id: str, prefs: dict[NotificationType, bool]) -> None` exists in `notifications.py`. When called, it writes or updates the entry for `user_id` in `_preferences`. Passing a key that is not a `NotificationType` member raises `ValueError`.

---

### Sub-task 3 — Guard `log_event()` against disabled preferences

**File:** `labs/lab-07-multi-agent-workflow/starter/notifications.py`

**Acceptance criterion:** `log_event()` calls `get_notification_preferences(user_id)` before appending to `_queue`. If the returned dict shows `False` for the given `event_type`, the function returns without appending. Existing behavior for enabled types is unchanged.

---

### Sub-task 4 — Write tests for sub-tasks 1 and 2

**File:** `labs/lab-07-multi-agent-workflow/starter/tests/test_notifications.py`

**Acceptance criterion:** At least one test for `get_notification_preferences()` and at least one test for `update_notification_preferences()` exist in `test_notifications.py`. Both tests pass when executed with `pytest`.

---

### Sub-task 5 — Write test for sub-task 3 (opt-out suppression)

**File:** `labs/lab-07-multi-agent-workflow/starter/tests/test_notifications.py`

**Acceptance criterion:** At least one test confirms that `log_event()` does not append to `_queue` when the user has set the relevant notification type to `False`. The test passes when executed with `pytest`.

---

## Hand-off Notes

- Sub-tasks 1, 2, and 3 must be completed in order — each session builds on the previous
- Sub-tasks 4 and 5 may be assigned to a Test Engineer session after all three implementation sub-tasks are complete
- Do not start sub-task 2 until sub-task 1 is committed — `update_notification_preferences()` writes to `_preferences` and benefits from having `get_notification_preferences()` already defined for validation in tests
